description:
Second subticket. Depends on .18.1.

Allow defmodule inside defmodule, producing dotted names:

  defmodule A do
    defmodule B do
      fn f(x), do: x
    end
  end

  fn main() do A.B.f(42) end

## Scope

- Parser: defmodule body accepts a nested defmodule as an item.
- AST: ModuleDef.items can include Item::Module recursively.
- Resolution pass: track current module path as a Vec<String>; produce qualified names like "A.B.f".
- Eval/load: same flat-env pattern as .18.1 — just longer names.

## Tests

- Single nested module callable from outside.
- Sibling resolution inside the inner module.
- Sibling resolution inside the outer module to one of its own fns NOT shadowed by inner module's same-named fn.
title: Nested modules
